fields.name: client_id
fields.title: Client ID
fields.description: Your HubSpot OAuth2 Client ID

fields.name: client_secret
fields.title: Client Secret
fields.description: Your HubSpot OAuth2 Client Secret

fields.name: refresh_token
fields.title: Refresh Token
fields.description: Your HubSpot OAuth2 Refresh Token

fields.name: access_token
fields.title: Access Token
fields.description: Your HubSpot OAuth2 Access Token (optional if refresh_token is provided)

fields.name: private_app_token
fields.title: Private App Token
fields.description: Access token from a HubSpot Private App

custom_description:
HubSpot is a CRM platform that provides tools for marketing, sales, customer service,
and content management. This connector provides access to contacts, companies, deals,
tickets, notes, calls, emails, meetings, tasks, and custom objects for customer relationship management and sales analytics.
supported_questions:
- List recent deals
- List recent tickets
- List companies in my CRM
- List contacts in my CRM
- Create a new contact with email john@example.com and name John Smith
- Create a new deal called 'Enterprise License' with amount 50000
- Update the deal stage to 'closedwon' for a specific deal
- Create a new company called 'Acme Corp' with domain acme.com
- Create a support ticket with subject 'Login issue' and priority HIGH
- Update the contact email for a specific contact
- Associate contact 123 with deal 456
- Link a contact to a company in HubSpot
- Set contact 123 as the Primary contact for company 456
- List all associations for contact 123 to companies
- Remove an association between a contact and a deal
- Add a note to contact 12345 saying 'Discussed pricing options'
- List recent notes in my CRM
- Get the details of a specific note
- Delete a note from HubSpot
- Log a call with contact 12345 about pricing discussion
- List recent calls in my CRM
- Create an email record for outreach to a contact
- List recent emails in my CRM
- Schedule a meeting with a contact for next Tuesday
- List recent meetings in my CRM
- Create a follow-up task for a deal
- List tasks in my CRM
- Show me all deals from Acme Corp this quarter
- What are the top 5 most valuable deals in my pipeline right now?
- Search for contacts in the marketing department at HubSpot
- Give me an overview of my sales team's deals in the last 30 days
- Identify the most active companies in our CRM this month
- Compare the number of deals closed by different sales representatives
- Find all tickets related to a specific product issue and summarize their status
unsupported_questions:
- Delete a contact from HubSpot
- Delete a deal record

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the paging.next.after JSON property of a paged response containing more results.

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of associated object types to include in the response. Valid values are contacts, deals, tickets, and custom object type IDs or fully qualified names (e.g., "p12345_cars").

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored.

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of companies that can be read by a single request.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived.

hubspot_owner_id.description: ID of the HubSpot owner to assign to this contact

notes.description: Returns a paginated list of notes

parameters.name: properties

hs_note_body.description: The body content of the note (supports HTML)

hs_timestamp.description: Required. Timestamp when the note activity occurred (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z). Use the current time if the user does not specify one.

hubspot_owner_id.description: ID of the HubSpot owner to assign to this note

parameters.description: Note properties to set

parameters.name: associations

schema.description: Associate the note with other CRM records (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)

id.description: ID of the record to associate with

associationTypeId.description: Association type ID (e.g., 202 for note-to-contact, 190 for note-to-company, 214 for note-to-deal, 18 for note-to-ticket)

parameters.description: Associate the note with other CRM records (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)

notes.description:
Create a new note in HubSpot CRM. Notes can be associated with contacts,
companies, deals, or tickets by using the associations parameter.
The hs_timestamp property sets when the note activity occurred.

parameters.description: Note ID

parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored.

parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored.

parameters.name: noteId
parameters.description: Note ID

notes.description:
Archive a note by ID. This is a soft delete — the note is moved to the
recycle bin and can be restored for approximately 90 days. No public
hard-delete endpoint exists.

calls.description: Returns a paginated list of calls

parameters.name: properties

hs_call_body.description: Description or notes about the call

hs_call_direction.description: Direction of the call (INBOUND or OUTBOUND)

hs_call_disposition.description: The outcome of the call (e.g., connected, no answer, busy, left voicemail)

hs_call_duration.description: Duration of the call in milliseconds

hs_call_from_number.description: Phone number the call was made from

hs_call_to_number.description: Phone number the call was made to

hs_call_status.description: Status of the call (e.g., COMPLETED, BUSY, NO_ANSWER, FAILED, CANCELED)

hs_call_title.description: Title or subject of the call

hs_timestamp.description: Required. Timestamp when the call activity occurred (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z). Use the current time if the user does not specify one.

hubspot_owner_id.description: ID of the HubSpot owner to assign to this call

parameters.description: Call properties to set

parameters.name: associations

schema.description: Associate the call with other CRM records (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)

id.description: ID of the record to associate with

associationTypeId.description: Association type ID (e.g., 194 for call-to-contact, 182 for call-to-company, 206 for call-to-deal, 220 for call-to-ticket)

parameters.description: Associate the call with other CRM records (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)

calls.description:
Create a new call engagement in HubSpot CRM. Calls can be associated with contacts,
companies, deals, or tickets by using the associations parameter.
The hs_timestamp property sets when the call activity occurred.

emails.description: Returns a paginated list of emails

parameters.name: properties

hs_email_text.description: Plain text body of the email

hs_email_direction.description: Required. Direction of the email (EMAIL for sent, INCOMING_EMAIL for received, FORWARDED_EMAIL for forwarded)

hs_email_status.description: Status of the email (BOUNCED, FAILED, SCHEDULED, SENDING, SENT, DRAFT)

hs_timestamp.description: Required. Timestamp when the email activity occurred (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z). Use the current time if the user does not specify one.

hubspot_owner_id.description: ID of the HubSpot owner to assign to this email

parameters.description: Associate the email with other CRM records (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)

Archive an email by ID. This is a soft delete — the email is moved to the
recycle bin and can be restored for approximately 90 days. No public
hard-delete endpoint exists.

hs_meeting_body.description: Description or notes about the meeting (supports HTML)

hs_meeting_end_time.description: End time of the meeting (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-15T11:30:00.000Z)

hs_meeting_outcome.description: Outcome of the meeting (e.g., SCHEDULED, COMPLETED, RESCHEDULED, NO_SHOW, CANCELED)

hs_task_priority.description: Priority of the task (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH)

hs_task_type.description: Type of the task (TODO, CALL, EMAIL). Defaults to TODO.

hs_timestamp.description: Required. Due date / timestamp for the task (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z). Use the current time if the user does not specify one.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page (default 500, max 500)

associations.description:
Retrieve all associations between a specific CRM record and a target object type using
the v4 associations API. Returns up to 500 associations per call. Use the `after` cursor
for pagination when there are more results. For example, retrieve all companies associated
with a contact, or all deals associated with a company.

schema.description: Paging cursor token for retrieving the next page
URL for retrieving the next page of results

parameters.name: associationCategory

schema.description:
Category of the association type. Use HUBSPOT_DEFINED for standard HubSpot association
types (e.g., primary company, default contact-to-deal) or USER_DEFINED for custom
association labels created in your HubSpot portal.

parameters.description:
Category of the association type. Use HUBSPOT_DEFINED for standard HubSpot association
types (e.g., primary company, default contact-to-deal) or USER_DEFINED for custom
association labels created in your HubSpot portal.

parameters.name: associationTypeId

schema.description:
Numeric identifier for the association type. Common IDs include:
279 = Contact to Company (default), 280 = Company to Contact (default),
4 = Contact to Deal (default), 3 = Deal to Contact (default),
341 = Deal to Company (default), 342 = Company to Deal (default),
1 = Contact to Primary Company, 2 = Company to Primary Contact,
5 = Deal to Primary Company, 6 = Primary Company to Deal,
15 = Contact to Ticket (default), 16 = Ticket to Contact (default),
339 = Ticket to Company (default), 340 = Company to Ticket (default),
26 = Ticket to Primary Company, 25 = Primary Company to Ticket.
Use the association definitions API to discover additional type IDs.

parameters.description:
Numeric identifier for the association type. Common IDs include:
279 = Contact to Company (default), 280 = Company to Contact (default),
4 = Contact to Deal (default), 3 = Deal to Contact (default),
341 = Deal to Company (default), 342 = Company to Deal (default),
1 = Contact to Primary Company, 2 = Company to Primary Contact,
5 = Deal to Primary Company, 6 = Primary Company to Deal,
15 = Contact to Ticket (default), 16 = Ticket to Contact (default),
339 = Ticket to Company (default), 340 = Company to Ticket (default),
26 = Ticket to Primary Company, 25 = Primary Company to Ticket.
Use the association definitions API to discover additional type IDs.

parameters.name: fromObjectType
parameters.description: Object type of the source record (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, or a custom object type ID)

parameters.name: fromObjectId
parameters.description: ID of the source record to associate from

parameters.name: toObjectType
parameters.description: Object type of the target record (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, or a custom object type ID)

parameters.name: toObjectId
parameters.description: ID of the target record to associate to

associations.description:
Create a labeled association between two CRM records using the v4 associations API.
Labeled associations carry an association type ID and category that describe the relationship
(e.g., "Primary Company", "Billing Contact"). This is idempotent — calling it again with the same
IDs and label has no effect. Use the association type ID and category from the HubSpot association
definitions for the relevant object pair. Common association type IDs include:
- Contact to Company: 279 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 1 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary
- Company to Contact: 280 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 2 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary
- Contact to Deal: 4 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default
- Deal to Contact: 3 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default
- Deal to Company: 341 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 5 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary
- Company to Deal: 342 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 6 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary
- Contact to Ticket: 15 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default
- Ticket to Contact: 16 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default
- Ticket to Company: 339 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 26 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary
- Company to Ticket: 340 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for default, 25 (HUBSPOT_DEFINED) for Primary

parameters.name: fromObjectType
parameters.description: Object type of the source record (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, or a custom object type ID)

parameters.name: fromObjectId
parameters.description: ID of the source record

parameters.name: toObjectType
parameters.description: Object type of the target record (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, or a custom object type ID)

parameters.name: toObjectId
parameters.description: ID of the target record

associations.description:
Delete all associations between two specific CRM records using the v4 associations API.
This removes every association (both default and labeled) between the two specified records.
This operation is irreversible — deleted associations must be recreated manually.
